description:
Discovered while landing fz-ul4.27.22.3. sum_list test fails 186577
vs expected 15 due to cont chain seam mismatch.

## Symptom

  fn sum([]), do: 0
  fn sum([h | t]), do: h + sum(t)
  fn main(), do: sum([1, 2, 3, 4, 5])

Test asserts 15 but gets 186577. The chain runs without crashing
but the wrong value lands in halt_value.

## Root cause

cont fn k_2 has its slot0 keyed by cont_input_key from sum's
TermCall site. cont_input_key sees the DIRECT producer
(sum_s3.effective_return = literal 0 → RawInt). Spec resolves to
k_2 spec with @abi (RawInt, ...) -> Tagged.

But k_2 is also reached via outer_cont chaining: when an INNER
k_2 calls its outer_cont (also a k_2), the inner k_2's return is
Tagged (h + sum_result widens to int|float → Tagged). The outer
k_2's slot0 then receives Tagged at runtime even though its sig
declares RawInt.

So slot0's true producer set = {sum's direct return, every cont
that chains its return through this cont's outer_cont}.

## ELI5

cont_input_key is "what does this cont's slot0 see at the seam,
given who built me?" But "who built me" only captures the
SYNCHRONOUS producer (the Term::Call whose continuation = this).
It misses the ASYNCHRONOUS producer: when k_2 itself ends with
Term::Return, that return targets outer_cont, which may itself be
another k_2 instance. The inner k_2's return repr flows into the
outer k_2's slot0.

For non-recursive cont chains this happens to align (the inner
cont's return = the user's last operation's repr, which the
outer cont was probably built to handle). For recursive cont
chains (sum_list, fold-style) the inner ks' returns widen via
arithmetic JOIN while the outer k_2 was keyed before that
widening was visible.

## Where the fix lives

Typer (ir_typer.rs): cont_input_key + cont keying need to JOIN
slot0 with "every fn F whose Term::Return targets this cont".
Equivalently: any cont spec c reached as outer_cont of some
producer F contributes F's return_repr to c's slot0.

Detection: walk every spec's body, every cont closure
construction site. Record: c.fn_id → set of upstream fn specs
whose return reaches c. Then c's slot0 JOIN includes all upstream
return reprs.

A subtle case: when sum_s3 builds k_2 with outer_cont = k_2
(itself a k_2 closure passed in via cont_param), the OUTER k_2 is
the one whose slot0 will receive inner k_2's return at chain
time. So:
  - inner k_2 builds outer k_2 (= the cont_param it received)
  - inner k_2.return_repr → outer k_2.slot0

Tracking this requires per-spec cont-construction analysis.

## Workaround (in 22.3 landing commit)

Codegen tagged_slot0_cont_specs catches: closure-target callees,
Receive, unknown CallClosure. Does NOT catch cont-as-outer-cont
chains. sum_list falls into this gap.

## Acceptance

1. sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion passes (returns 15).
2. The cont seam in sum_list's k_2 is Tagged at slot0 (not
   RawInt) because chain analysis surfaces the Tagged upstream
   producer.
3. No regression on the 452 tests that pass today.

## Notes

May also affect: fold-style fns, mutual cont recursion, any cont
that's both built (as a cont) AND inherited (via cont_param
chaining) within the same fn.

comments.text:
Resolved by fz-3zx + fz-ntz (2026-05-14).

Root-cause fix landed in two parts:
  - fz-3zx switched the typer to a real Kleene LFP for spec
    effective returns, retiring cycle-cut effective_return_descr.
    Cont slot 0 keys are now derived from the LFP cache.
  - fz-ntz added tagged_return_fns: the transitive closure of fns
    whose return is Tagged-by-construction (closure-target seeds
    + TailCallClosure terminators, propagated through TailCall AND
    Call/CallClosure/Receive continuations). The propagation
    through Call/Call cont is EXACTLY the chain-via-outer_cont
    case this ticket flagged as a gap in 22.3's workaround.

Verification:
  - sum_list_via_head_tail_recursion: 15 ✓ (was 186577)
  - All 459 lib tests pass.
  - higher_order.fz JIT: 42 -7 -10 ✓ (was 337 -55 -79 mid-landing).

Closing.

title: fz-cont-chain-seam: cont's slot0 must JOIN all chain producers
